text: During exercise lasting approximately 1 hour, a carbohydrate mouth rinse or small amounts of carbohydrate ingestion can improve performance.
A single carbohydrate source can be oxidized at rates up to approximately 60 g/h, which is the recommended intake for prolonged exercise lasting 2-3 hours.
For ultra-endurance events, carbohydrate intake should be approximately 90 g/h using multiple transportable carbohydrates to achieve high oxidation rates.
High carbohydrate ingestion rates (>60 g/h) require multiple transportable carbohydrates (e.g., glucose + fructose) to prevent gastrointestinal accumulation and allow high oxidation rates.
Carbohydrate intake recommendations during exercise are independent of body weight and training status.
The form of carbohydrate during exercise (liquid, semisolid, or solid) does not meaningfully affect recommendations, though intake should be adjusted downward when absolute exercise intensity is low.
Carbohydrate fueling guidelines should be personalized based on exercise duration and intensity rather than applying a single generic recommendation.
